articles.title: Maharashtra heatwave alert: Temperatures in Vidarbha reach 45 degrees Celsius as parts of state continue to witness hot weather - Mid-day
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Maharashtra
articles.full_text:
Most parts of Maharashtra are currently witnessing hot weather conditions. Representational Pic/File
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Saturday predicted hot weather conditions in parts of Maharashtra with the Vidarbha region recording maximum temperatures likely to touch 45 degrees Celsius.
According to the weather department, Nagpur and Wardha are likely to experience some of the highest temperatures, touching around 44 degrees Celsius to 45 degrees Celsius.
Clear skies are expected for most of the week, which will contribute to rising daytime temperatures and warm nights.
A weather bulletin issued by the Nagpur Regional Meteorological Centre stated that while most districts will see clear or partly cloudy skies, some areas such as Chandrapur and Gadchiroli may experience occasional thunderstorms. However, these are not expected to bring significant relief from the heat.
Cities including Bhandara, Gondia, Amravati, Akola, Yavatmal, Buldhana, and Washim are also forecast to face high temperatures ranging between 41 degrees Celsius and 45 degrees Celsius.
Night temperatures are expected to remain between 24 degrees Celsius and 27 degrees Celsius, adding to discomfort levels.
The forecast suggested that heatwave conditions will persist for the next few days, with only minor variations in temperature. Weather officials have not indicated any major cooling trend in the near future.
In an advisory, issued by the weather department earlier this week, below are the expected impacts from heatwave conditions and hot and humid weather.
- Vulnerable people may take necessary precaution.
- It is advised to take precautionary measures for hot weather while going outside during peak hours of the day.
- Heat cramp, heat rash likely during peak hours of the day.
Action suggested:
- Avoid prolonged heat exposure.
- Stay hydrated. Drink sufficient water even if not feeling thirsty to avoid dehydration.
- Wear light weight, light colored, loose, cotton clothes.
- Cover your head: use a wet cloth, hat or umbrella while going out during peak hours.
- Caution workers to avoid direct sunlight during the peak hours.
- Schedule strenuous jobs to cooler times of the day.
- Increasing the frequency and length of rest breaks for outdoor activities.
- Recognise the signs of heat stroke, heat rash or heat cramps such as weakness, dizziness, headache, nausea, sweating and seizures. If you feel faint or ill, see a doctor/ hospital immediately.
- Farmers are advised to continue irrigation activities.

articles.title: Washim Heat Stroke: Washim District Shaken! Temperature Surpasses 43 Degrees Celsius, Youth Dies from Heatstroke - Lokshahi English News
articles.full_text:
Washim Heat Stroke: Washim District Shaken! Temperature Surpasses 43 Degrees Celsius, Youth Dies from Heatstroke
The scorching summer is intensifying. The sun is blazing, and a heat wave is spreading. As temperatures rise daily, the number of heatstroke patients is also rapidly increasing. This time, more than 15 heatstroke cases have been reported in the state. The number of patients is now increasing in Gadchiroli and Jalna districts. Reports have emerged of deaths due to heatstroke in Akola and Washim.
In Washim, the heat has intensified, with temperatures surpassing 43 degrees Celsius. Due to this elevated temperature, a young man's death has been reported in the hilly areas of Washim. The deceased has been identified as 25-year-old Gopal Thakre. This young man was helping in his field to grind turmeric for the past two days. He collapsed due to dizziness in the afternoon. Later, he was admitted to a hospital in Malegaon. However, his condition worsened, and after being transferred to a private hospital in Washim, he was declared dead. The youth's death has caused sorrow in the hilly Panchkroshi region.
The intensity of the heat in the state shows that in all twelve districts of Vidarbha, the temperature has crossed 42 degrees Celsius. The highest temperature recorded was 44 degrees Celsius in Akola and Amravati. In the Marathwada region, Beed district recorded 43.1 degrees Celsius. Approximately 23 districts in the state recorded maximum temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius on Wednesday. In the central Maharashtra district of Jalgaon, 42 degrees Celsius was recorded. Meanwhile, Malegaon's temperature has reached 43 degrees Celsius. In western Maharashtra, the temperatures in Satara, Sangli, and Solapur districts have all exceeded 40 degrees, with the highest being 44 degrees in Amravati and 43.3 degrees in Solapur. Satara recorded 40.9 degrees and Sangli 41.8 degrees, while 43.9 degrees was noted.
The health department has urged citizens to go outside only when necessary during the afternoon and to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated.
